title: ED fracture suspicion documentation

examination.text: Supports deformity, distal pulse, capillary refill, sensation, neurovascular compromise, open fracture, splinting, and perfusion reassessment documentation.
examination.rationale: The exact WHO/ICRC extremity-trauma section supports recording vital data, appearance and time-critical abnormalities only when assessed; it does not auto-state neurovascular status.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports deformity, distal pulse, capillary refill, sensation, neurovascular compromise, open fracture, splinting, and perfusion reassessment documentation.

source.title: Basic emergency care: approach to the acutely ill and injured

investigations.text: Covers non-complex fracture assessment and follow-up, repeated pain assessment, definitive X-ray reporting, Ottawa ankle and foot rules, and selected scaphoid imaging.
investigations.rationale: The exact NICE non-complex-fracture assessment section supports mechanism, onset, severity, associated symptoms, relevant negatives, risk context and manual focused assessment without confirming a fracture.
investigations.evidence_extract: Covers non-complex fracture assessment and follow-up, repeated pain assessment, definitive X-ray reporting, Ottawa ankle and foot rules, and selected scaphoid imaging.

investigations.text: Requires structured fracture documentation, image and report continuity, honest information, questions, treatment expectations, rehabilitation, mobilisation, weight-bearing, management-plan communication, and documentation.
investigations.rationale: The exact documentation and information section supports recording existing imaging, clinician plan, completed discussion and arranged handover only as clinician-confirmed facts.
investigations.evidence_extract: Requires structured fracture documentation, image and report continuity, honest information, questions, treatment expectations, rehabilitation, mobilisation, weight-bearing, management-plan communication, and documentation.

referral.text: Supports selected orthopaedic follow-up, stability review, weight-bearing, cast or fixation context, and return review for non-improving symptoms in specified fracture populations.
referral.rationale: Management recommendations were reviewed only to delimit clinician-documented actions; no immobilisation, analgesia, manipulation or referral is generated.
referral.evidence_extract: Supports selected orthopaedic follow-up, stability review, weight-bearing, cast or fixation context, and return review for non-improving symptoms in specified fracture populations.

source_limitations: Anatomical site, deformity, skin, neurovascular detail, open injury, imaging choice and result, diagnosis, treatment, procedure, medicine, dose and disposition remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
